reference: Hi, I am ten weeks pregnant, and I have been experiencing a lot of lower abdominal pain that lasts for long periods of time. It always starts when I am standing at work for a while. I went to my doctor the other day, and we discovered that I have a retroverted uterus. Can that really be causing a lot of pain, or should I be worried? I am not sure if I should wait it out or seek help now. I have an ultrasound scheduled for the 21st.
text: Hi, I am really pregnant and I have been experiencing a lot of lower abdominal pain the past few days. I know it starts around the beginning of the third trimester, but what can I do to relieve it? Can you give me calming waters, ocean, wood? I am not sure if I should wait it out or seek help now. Can I have an ultrasound scheduled on the first?
